mission.title: vitess: Using DUAL in `INSERT ... SELECT` unsharded subquery returns `unsupported sharded subquery in insert values`
mission.description: Using DUAL in `INSERT ... SELECT` unsharded subquery returns `unsupported sharded subquery in insert values`. This issue affects 6+ users.

steps.title: Identify vitess troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your vitess deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n vitess -l app.kubernetes.io/name=vitess
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=vitess -n vitess --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review vitess configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant vitess configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n vitess -l app.kubernetes.io/name=vitess
kubectl get configmap -n vitess -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=vitess
```
Adding a reference to `DUAL` in an `INSERT ... SELECT` query, where the keyspace is unsharded, makes VTGate think the query is cross-shard and fail with `unsupported: sharded subquery in insert values`.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Using DUAL in `INSERT ... SELECT` unsharded subquery
steps.description:
This PR adds support for planning and executing `INSERT` queries that take their input from a `SELECT` or `UNION` query.

```sql
insert into ... select query ...
```
```yaml
This works:
```

steps.title: Confirm Using DUAL in `INSERT ... SELECT` unsharded is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=vitess -n vitess --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n vitess --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
This PR adds support for planning and executing `INSERT` queries that take their input from a `SELECT` or `UNION` query.

```sql
insert into ... select query ...
```

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with vitess installed or the issue environment reproducible.
